title: calonemorchis incrassata
intro: Calonemorchis incrassata, also known as the thickened calonemorchis or thick-leaved calonemorchis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Australia and New Guinea. It is primarily used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating wounds. In traditional herbal preparations, Calonemorchis incrassata is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
